[Fixing problems building 32 bit binaries](#fixing-problems-building-32-bit-binaries)\\n  - [Allocator](#allocator)\\n  - [Monotonic clock](#monotonic-clock)\\n  - [Verbose build](#verbose-build)\\n  - [Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-09T15:42:57.010164553Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

[Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-09T15:42:57.010164553Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}

AlmaLinux 9.7+ / Rocky Linux 9.7+](#build-and-run-redis-with-all-data-structures---almalinux-97--rocky-linux-97)\\n  - [Build and run Redis with all data structures - AlmaLinux 10.1+ / Rocky Linux 10.1+](#build-and-run-redis-with-all-data-structures---almalinux-101--rocky-linux-101)\\n  - [Build and run Redis with all data structures - Alpine 3.23+](#build-and-run-redis-with-all-data-structures---alpine-323)\\n  - [Build and run Redis with all data structures - macOS 14 (Sonoma), 15 (Sequoia), 26 (Tahoe)](#build-and-run-redis-with-all-data-structures---macos-14-sonoma-15-sequoia-26-tahoe)\\n  - [Building Redis - flags and general notes](#building-redis---flags-and-general-notes)\\n  - [Fixing build problems with dependencies or cached build options](#fixing-build-problems-with-dependencies-or-cached-build-options)\\n  - [Fixing problems building 32 bit binaries](#fixing-problems-building-32-bit-binaries)\\n  - [Allocator](#allocator)\\n  - [Monotonic clock](#monotonic-clock)\\n  - [Verbose build](#verbose-build)\\n  - [Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-09T15:43:25.091725795Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

[Running Redis with TLS](#running-redis-with-tls)\\n- [Code contributions](#code-contributions)\\n- [Redis Trademarks](#redis-trademarks)\\n\\n## What is Redis?\\n\\nFor developers, who are building real-time data-driven applications, Redis is the preferred, fastest, and most feature-rich cache, data structure server, and document and vector query engine.\\n\\n### Key use cases\\n\\nRedis excels in various applications, including:\\n\\n- **Caching:** Supports multiple eviction policies, key expiration, and hash-field expiration.\\n- **Distributed Session Store:** Offers flexible session data modeling (string, JSON, hash).\\n- **Data Structure Server:** Provides low-level data structures (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, JSON, etc.) with high-level semantics (counters, queues, leaderboards, rate limiters) and supports transactions & scripting.\\n- **NoSQL Data Store:** Key-value, document, and time series data storage.\\n- **Search and Query Engine:** Indexing for hash/JSON documents, supporting vector search, full-text search, geospatial queries, ranking, and aggregations via Redis Search.\\n- **Event Store & Message Broker:** Implements queues (lists), priority queues (sorted sets), event deduplication (sets), streams, and pub/sub with probabilistic stream processing capabilities.\\n- **Vector Store for GenAI:** Integrates with AI applications (e.g. LangGraph, mem0) for short-term memory, long-term memory, LLM response caching (semantic caching), and retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\\n- **Real-Time Analytics:** Powers personalization, recommendations, fraud detection, and risk assessment.\\n\\n## Why choose Redis?\\n\\nRedis is a popular choice for developers worldwide due to its combination of speed, flexibility, and rich feature set. Here's why people choose Redis for:\\n\\n- **Performance:** Because Redis keeps data primarily in memory and uses efficient data structures, it achieves extremely low latency (often sub-millisecond) for both read and write operations. This makes it ideal for applications demanding real-time responsiveness.\\n- **Flexibility:** Redis isn't just a key-value store, it provides native support for a wide range of data structures and capabilities listed in [What is Redis?](#what-is-redis)\\n- **Extensibility:** Redis is not limited to the built-in data structures, it has a [modules API](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/reference/modules/) that makes it possible to extend Redis functionality and rapidly implement new Redis commands\\n- **Simplicity:** Redis has a simple, text-based protocol and [well-documented command set](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/)\\n- **Ubiquity:** Redis is battle tested in production workloads at a massive scale. There is a good chance you indirectly interact with Redis several times daily\\n- **Versatility**: Redis is the de facto standard for use cases such as:\\n  - **Caching:** quickly access frequently used data without needing to query your primary database\\n  - **Session management:** read and write user session data without hurting user experience or slowing down every API call\\n  - **Querying, sorting, and analytics:** perform deduplication, full text search, and secondary indexing on in-memory data as fast as possible\\n  - **Messaging and interservice communication:** job queues, message brokering, pub/sub, and streams for communicating between services\\n  - **Vector operations:** Long-term and short-term LLM memory, RAG content retrieval, semantic caching, semantic routing, and vector similarity search\\n\\nIn summary, Redis provides a powerful, fast, and flexible toolkit for solving a wide variety of data management challenges. If you want to know more, here is a list of starting points:\\n\\n- [**Introduction to Redis data types**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/)\\n- [**The full list of Redis commands**](https://redis.io/commands/)\\n- [**Redis for AI**](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/ai/)\\n- [**Redis documentation**](https://redis.io/documentation/)\\n\\n## What is Redis Open Source?\\n\\nRedis Community Edition (Redis CE) was renamed Redis Open Source with the v8.0 release.\\n\\nRedis Ltd. also offers [Redis Software](https://redis.io/enterprise/), a self-managed software with additional compliance, reliability, and resiliency for enterprise scaling,\\nand [Redis Cloud](https://redis.io/cloud/), a fully managed service integrated with Google Cloud, Azure, and AWS for production-ready apps.\\n\\nRead more about the differences between Redis Open Source and Redis [here](https:/...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-09T15:43:25.091725795Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}

Quick starts Client tools Client APIs Using commands Data types Compare data types Strings Arrays Geospatial Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets Redis for AI and search Programmability Use cases Pub/sub Reference Libraries and tools Redis products Commands Docs Docs → Develop with Redis → Redis data types Redis data types Overview of data types supported by Redis Redis is a data structure server. At its core, Redis provides a collection of native data types that help you solve a wide variety of problems, from caching to queuing to event processing . Below is a short description of each data type, with links to broader overviews and command references. Each overview includes a comprehensive tutorial with code samples. Data types Redis Open Source implements the following data types: Strings Bitmaps Bitfields Arrays Geospatial indexes Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic data types Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets See Compare data types for advice on which of the general-purpose data types is best for common tasks. Strings Redis strings are the most basic Redis data type, representing a sequence of bytes. For more information, see: Overview of Redis strings Redis string command reference Bitfields Redis bitfields efficiently encode multiple counters in a string value. Bitfields provide atomic get, set, and increment operations and support different overflow policies. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitfields The BITFIELD command. Bitmaps Redis bitmaps let you perform bitwise operations on strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitmaps Redis bitmap command reference Arrays Redis arrays are sparse, index-addressable sequences of strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis arrays Redis array command reference Geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes are useful for finding locations within a given geographic radius or bounding box. For more information, see: Overview of Redis geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes command reference Hashes Redis hashes are record types modeled as collections of field-value pairs. As such, Redis hashes resemble Python dictionaries , Java HashMaps , and Ruby hashes . For more information, see: Overview of Redis hashes Redis hashes command reference JSON Redis JSON provides structured, hierarchical arrays and key-value objects that match the popular JSON text file format. You can import JSON text into Redis objects and access, modify, and query individual data elements. For more information, see: Overview of Redis JSON JSON command reference Lists Redis lists are lists of strings sorted by insertion order. For more information, see: Overview of Redis lists Redis list command reference Probabilistic data types These data types let you gather and calculate statistics in a way that is approximate but highly efficient. The following types are available: Bloom filter Count-min sketch Cuckoo filter HyperLogLog t-digest Top-K Bloom filter Redis Bloom filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Bloom filters Bloom filter command reference Count-min sketch Redis Count-min sketch estimate the frequency of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Count-min sketch overview Count-min sketch command reference Cuckoo filter Redis Cuckoo filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. They are similar to Bloom filters but with slightly different trade-offs between features and performance. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Cuckoo filters Cuckoo filter command reference HyperLogLog The Redis HyperLogLog data structures provide probabilistic estimates of the cardinality (i.e., number of elements) of large sets. For more information, see: Overview of Redis HyperLogLog Redis HyperLogLog command reference t-digest Redis t-digest structures estimate percentiles from a stream of data values. For more information, see: Redis t-digest overview t-digest command reference Top-K Redis Top-K structures estimate the ranking of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Top-K overview Top-K command reference Sets Redis sets are unordered collections of unique strings that act like the sets from your favorite programming language (for example, Java HashSets , Python sets , and so on). With a Redis set, you can add, remove, and test for existence in O(1) time (in other words, regardless of the number of set elements). For more information, see: Overview of Redis sets Redis set command reference Sorted sets Redis sorted sets are collections of unique strings that maintain order by each string's associated score. For more information, see: Overview of Redis sorted sets Redis sorted set command reference Streams A Redis stream is a data structure that acts like an append-only log. Streams help record events in the order they occur and then syndicate them for processing. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Streams Redis Streams command reference Time series Redis time series structures let you store and query timestamped data points. For more information, see: Redis time series overview Time series command reference Vector sets Redis vector sets are a specialized data type designed for managing high-dimensional vector data, enabling fast and efficient vector similarity search within Redis. Vector sets are optimized for use cases involving machine learning, recommendation systems, and semantic search, where each vector represents a data point in multi-dimensional space. Vector sets supports the HNSW (hierarchical navigable small world) algorithm, allowing you to store, index, and query vectors based on the cosine similarity metric. With vector sets, Redis provides native support for hybrid search, combining vector similarity with structured filters . For more information, see: Overview of Redis vector sets Redis vector set command reference Adding extensions To extend the features provided by the included data types, use one of these options: Write your own custom server-side functions in Lua . Write your own Redis module using the modules API or check out the community-supported modules . Quick starts Client tools Client APIs Using commands Data types Compare data types Strings Arrays Geospatial Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets Redis for AI and search Programmability Use cases Pub/sub Reference Libraries and tools Redis products Commands Docs Docs → Develop with Redis → Redis data types Redis data types Overview of data types supported by Redis Redis is a data structure server. At its core, Redis provides a collection of native data types that help you solve a wide variety of problems, from caching to queuing to event processing . Below is a short description of each data type, with links to broader overviews and command references. Each overview includes a comprehensive tutorial with code samples. Data types Redis Open Source implements the following data types: Strings Bitmaps Bitfields Arrays Geospatial indexes Hashes JSON Lists Probabilistic data types Sets Sorted sets Streams Time series Vector sets See Compare data types for advice on which of the general-purpose data types is best for common tasks. Strings Redis strings are the most basic Redis data type, representing a sequence of bytes. For more information, see: Overview of Redis strings Redis string command reference Bitfields Redis bitfields efficiently encode multiple counters in a string value. Bitfields provide atomic get, set, and increment operations and support different overflow policies. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitfields The BITFIELD command. Bitmaps Redis bitmaps let you perform bitwise operations on strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis bitmaps Redis bitmap command reference Arrays Redis arrays are sparse, index-addressable sequences of strings. For more information, see: Overview of Redis arrays Redis array command reference Geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes are useful for finding locations within a given geographic radius or bounding box. For more information, see: Overview of Redis geospatial indexes Redis geospatial indexes command reference Hashes Redis hashes are record types modeled as collections of field-value pairs. As such, Redis hashes resemble Python dictionaries , Java HashMaps , and Ruby hashes . For more information, see: Overview of Redis hashes Redis hashes command reference JSON Redis JSON provides structured, hierarchical arrays and key-value objects that match the popular JSON text file format. You can import JSON text into Redis objects and access, modify, and query individual data elements. For more information, see: Overview of Redis JSON JSON command reference Lists Redis lists are lists of strings sorted by insertion order. For more information, see: Overview of Redis lists Redis list command reference Probabilistic data types These data types let you gather and calculate statistics in a way that is approximate but highly efficient. The following types are available: Bloom filter Count-min sketch Cuckoo filter HyperLogLog t-digest Top-K Bloom filter Redis Bloom filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Bloom filters Bloom filter command reference Count-min sketch Redis Count-min sketch estimate the frequency of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Count-min sketch overview Count-min sketch command reference Cuckoo filter Redis Cuckoo filters let you check for the presence or absence of an element in a set. They are similar to Bloom filters but with slightly different trade-offs between features and performance. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Cuckoo filters Cuckoo filter command reference HyperLogLog The Redis HyperLogLog data structures provide probabilistic estimates of the cardinality (i.e., number of elements) of large sets. For more information, see: Overview of Redis HyperLogLog Redis HyperLogLog command reference t-digest Redis t-digest structures estimate percentiles from a stream of data values. For more information, see: Redis t-digest overview t-digest command reference Top-K Redis Top-K structures estimate the ranking of a data point within a stream of values. For more information, see: Redis Top-K overview Top-K command reference Sets Redis sets are unordered collections of unique strings that act like the sets from your favorite programming language (for example, Java HashSets , Python sets , and so on). With a Redis set, you can add, remove, and test for existence in O(1) time (in other words, regardless of the number of set elements). For more information, see: Overview of Redis sets Redis set command reference Sorted sets Redis sorted sets are collections of unique strings that maintain order by each string's associated score. For more information, see: Overview of Redis sorted sets Redis sorted set command reference Streams A Redis stream is a data structure that acts like an append-only log. Streams help record events in the order they occur and then syndicate them for processing. For more information, see: Overview of Redis Streams Redis Streams command reference Time series Redis time series structures let you store and query timestamped data points. For more information, see: Redis time series overview Time series command reference Vector sets Redis vector sets are a specialized data type designed for managing high-dimensional vector data, enabling fast and efficient vector similarity search within Redis. Vector sets are optimized for use cases involving machine learning, recommendation systems, and semantic search, where each vector represents a data point in multi-dimensional space. Vector sets supports the HNSW (hierarchical navigable small world) algorithm, allowing you to store, index, and query vectors based on the cosine similarity metric. With vector sets, Redis provides native support for hybrid search, combining vector similarity with structured filters . For more information, see: Overview of Redis vector sets Redis vector set command reference Adding extensions To extend the features provided by the included data types, use one of these options: Write your own custom server-side functions in Lua . Write your own Redis module using the modules API or check out the community-supported modules . Redis 开源内存数据结构存储引擎深度解析\\n\\n## 核心定位与架构\\nRedis 是一个高性能的**内存数据结构服务器**。其核心优势在于将数据存储在内存中，从而提供极致的读写速度，同时通过多种机制保证数据的持久性和高可用性。\\n\\n*   **高性能**：实现亚毫秒级延迟，适合高并发场景。\\n*   **丰富数据结构**：不仅支持基础类型，还扩展了复杂数据结构。\\n*   **灵活扩展**：通过 Modules API 和内置扩展模块，支持多种高级功能。\\n\\n## 支持的数据类型\\nRedis 提供了多样化的数据结构，以满足不同业务场景的需求：\\n\\n*   **基础类型**：字符串 (String)、哈希 (Hash)、列表 (List)、集合 (Set)、有序集合 (ZSet)。\\n*   **高级/扩展类型**：\\n    *   **JSON**：支持原生 JSON 文档存储与查询。\\n    *   **流 (Stream)**：用于消息队列和日志处理。\\n    *   **时间序列 (Time Series)**：专为 IoT 和监控数据设计。\\n    *   **向量集合 (Vector Set)**：支持向量相似度搜索，服务于 GenAI 场景。\\n    *   **布隆过滤器 (Bloom Filter)**：用于快速判断元素是否存在，节省空间。\\n\\n## 关键特性与技术能力\\n\\n### 1. 数据持久化\\n为防止内存数据丢失，Redis 提供了双重持久化机制：\\n*   **RDB (Redis Database)**：生成数据快照，适合备份和灾难恢复。\\n*   **AOF (Append Only File)**：记录写操作日志，提供更细粒度的数据恢复能力。\\n\\n### 2. 高可用与扩展性\\n*   **主从复制 (Replication)**：实现数据的多副本同步，提升读取性能和容灾能力。\\n*   **集群分片 (Cluster Sharding)**：支持数据自动分片，实现水平扩展，处理海量数据和高吞吐需求。\\n\\n### 3. 高级功能扩展\\n*   **Lua 脚本**：支持原子性执行复杂逻辑，确保数据一致性。\\n*   **Modules API**：允许开发者编写 C 语言模块，扩展 Redis 内核功能。\\n*   **内置扩展模块**：\\n    *   **Redis Search**：提供全文检索能力。\\n    *   **Redis Vector**：支持向量相似度搜索。\\n    *   **Redis Time Series**：优化时间序列数据的存储与查询。\\n    *   **Redis Bloom Filter**：提供高效的概率型数据结构。\\n\\n## 典型应用场景\\n基于其高性能和丰富的数据类型，Redis 广泛应用于以下领域：\\n\\n*   **缓存**：作为应用层的高速缓存，减轻后端数据库压力。\\n*   **会话存储**：存储用户会话信息，支持快速读写。\\n*   **消息队列**：利用 List 或 Stream 结构实现异步任务处理。\\n*   **实时分析**：结合时间序列和聚合操作，进行实时数据监控。\\n*   **GenAI 向量存储**：利用向量集合功能，存储和检索嵌入向量，支持大模型应用。\\n\\n## 总结\\nRedis 凭借其**亚毫秒级延迟**、**丰富的数据结构**以及**强大的扩展生态**，已成为现代软件架构中不可或缺的基础组件。无论是传统的缓存场景，还是新兴的 AI 向量存储需求，Redis 都能提供高效、可靠的解决方案。\"}","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-09T15:44:50.934671267Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

Today we are diving into Redis, the open-source, in-memory data structure store. We will explore its architecture, supported data types, and key features that make it indispensable for modern software development. This presentation is tailored for developers looking for implementation details and technical decision-makers evaluating infrastructure choices.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA sleek, modern 3D render of a glowing blue database cylinder with interconnected nodes, symbolizing speed and connectivity, set against a dark tech background.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 2: What is Redis?\\n\\n**Title:** Redis: The In-Memory Data Store\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Definition:** A high-performance, open-source server for data structures.\\n*   **Primary Medium:** Data is stored primarily in memory for ultra-low latency.\\n*   **Versatility:** Supports strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, JSON, streams, time series, and vector collections.\\n*   **Open Source:** Community-driven with robust enterprise support options.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis is fundamentally an in-memory data structure server. Unlike traditional disk-based databases, Redis keeps data in RAM, enabling sub-millisecond response times. It is not just a key-value store; it is a rich data structure server supporting complex types like JSON, streams, and vectors, making it suitable for a wide range of use cases from caching to real-time analytics.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nAn infographic comparing a slow disk-based database (with a clock icon) vs. a fast in-memory Redis store (with a lightning bolt icon), highlighting the speed difference.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 3: Core Data Structures\\n\\n**Title:** Rich Data Types for Complex Use Cases\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Strings:** Basic key-value storage, counters, and flags.\\n*   **Hashes:** Object-like structures mapping fields to values.\\n*   **Lists:** Ordered collections of strings (queues, timelines).\\n*   **Sets:** Unordered unique collections (tagging, social graphs).\\n*   **Sorted Sets:** Unique items with scores (leaderboards, priority queues).\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis shines because of its diverse data structures. Strings are the simplest form, but Hashes allow you to store objects. Lists are perfect for message queues or recent activity feeds. Sets handle unique items efficiently, while Sorted Sets add a scoring mechanism, ideal for leaderboards or scheduling tasks based on priority.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA visual diagram showing five distinct icons representing Strings, Hashes, Lists, Sets, and Sorted Sets, each with a small example of data inside (e.g., a leaderboard for Sorted Sets).\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 4: Advanced Data Types\\n\\n**Title:** Modern Data Needs: JSON, Streams, and Vectors\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **JSON:** Native JSON support for document-like storage without parsing overhead.\\n*   **Streams:** Append-only log for event sourcing and message streaming.\\n*   **Time Series:** Optimized for storing and querying time-stamped data.\\n*   **Vector Collections:** Support for high-dimensional vectors for AI/ML applications.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nBeyond basic structures, Redis supports modern data needs. You can store and query JSON documents natively. Streams provide an append-only log for event-driven architectures. Time Series modules handle IoT and monitoring data efficiently. Crucially, Vector Collections enable semantic search and AI applications by storing high-dimensional vectors for similarity searches.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA conceptual illustration of a JSON document being stored in a Redis key, alongside a timeline graph for time series data and a 3D vector space for AI embeddings.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 5: Performance Characteristics\\n\\n**Title:** Sub-Millisecond Latency\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Speed:** In-memory architecture ensures sub-millisecond response times.\\n*   **Single-Threaded Core:** Avoids lock contention and context switching overhead.\\n*   **High Throughput:** Capable of handling millions of operations per second.\\n*   **Predictable Performance:** Consistent latency regardless of data volume (within memory limits).\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nThe primary advantage of Redis is speed. Because data resides in RAM, operations are incredibly fast. Redis uses a single-threaded event loop for core operations, which eliminates the overhead of thread locking and context switching, leading to predictable, low-latency performance even under heavy load.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA line graph showing latency on the Y-axis and time on the X-axis, with a flat line near zero representing Redis performance compared to a fluctuating line for traditional databases.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 6: Persistence Strategies\\n\\n**Title:** Ensuring Data Durability: RDB & AOF\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **RDB (Redis Database):** Point-in-time snapshots of the dataset.\\n    *   Efficient for backups and large-scale restores.\\n    *   Minimal impact on performance during fork.\\n*   **AOF (Append Only File):** Logs every write operation.\\n    *   Higher data durability (can lose last second of data).\\n    *   Better recoverability after crashes.\\n*   **Hybrid Approach:** Combine RDB and AOF for best of both worlds.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nWhile Redis is in-memory, it is not volatile. It offers two persistence mechanisms. RDB creates periodic snapshots, which are great for backups. AOF logs every write command, offering higher durability at the cost of larger file sizes. Many production environments use a hybrid approach to balance performance and data safety.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA split-screen diagram: Left side shows a snapshot icon (RDB) being taken periodically; Right side shows a continuous log file being written to (AOF).\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 7: High Availability & Replication\\n\\n**Title:** Scalability and Reliability\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Master-Replica Replication:** Asynchronous replication for read scaling and redundancy.\\n*   **Automatic Failover:** Sentinel monitors masters and promotes replicas automatically.\\n*   **Redis Cluster:** Native sharding for horizontal scaling.\\n    *   Data is partitioned across multiple nodes.\\n    *   Handles node failures gracefully.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis scales horizontally and vertically. Master-Replica replication allows you to offload read traffic to replicas. For high availability, Redis Sentinel automatically monitors nodes and promotes a replica if the master fails. For massive scale, Redis Cluster shards data across multiple nodes, ensuring the system remains available even if individual nodes go down.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA network topology diagram showing one Master node connected to multiple Replica nodes, with a Sentinel node monitoring them, and a separate Cluster view showing data sharded across multiple nodes.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 8: Advanced Features\\n\\n**Title:** Powering Complex Logic\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Lua Scripting:** Atomic execution of complex logic on the server side.\\n*   **Redis Modules:** Extensible architecture for custom data types and functions.\\n*   **Redis Search:** Full-text search capabilities with faceting and filtering.\\n*   **Redis Vector:** Vector similarity search for AI and recommendation engines.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis is extensible. You can write Lua scripts to execute multiple commands atomically, ensuring data consistency. The Modules API allows developers to extend Redis with custom data types. Redis Search provides powerful full-text search, while Redis Vector enables semantic search, making Redis a central hub for AI-driven applications.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA code snippet of a Lua script executing on a Redis server, surrounded by icons representing Search (magnifying glass) and Vector (neural network nodes).\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 9: Use Case...[truncated]","startedAt":null,"finishedAt":"2026-06-09T15:45:05.505761067Z","errorCode":null,"message":"SUCCEEDED"}}

Today we are diving into Redis, the open-source, in-memory data structure store. We will explore its architecture, supported data types, and key features that make it indispensable for modern software development. This presentation is tailored for developers looking for implementation details and technical decision-makers evaluating infrastructure choices.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA sleek, modern 3D render of a glowing blue database cylinder with interconnected nodes, symbolizing speed and connectivity, set against a dark tech background.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 2: What is Redis?\\n\\n**Title:** Redis: The In-Memory Data Store\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Definition:** A high-performance, open-source server for data structures.\\n*   **Primary Medium:** Data is stored primarily in memory for ultra-low latency.\\n*   **Versatility:** Supports strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, JSON, streams, time series, and vector collections.\\n*   **Open Source:** Community-driven with robust enterprise support options.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis is fundamentally an in-memory data structure server. Unlike traditional disk-based databases, Redis keeps data in RAM, enabling sub-millisecond response times. It is not just a key-value store; it is a rich data structure server supporting complex types like JSON, streams, and vectors, making it suitable for a wide range of use cases from caching to real-time analytics.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nAn infographic comparing a slow disk-based database (with a clock icon) vs. a fast in-memory Redis store (with a lightning bolt icon), highlighting the speed difference.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 3: Core Data Structures\\n\\n**Title:** Rich Data Types for Complex Use Cases\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Strings:** Basic key-value storage, counters, and flags.\\n*   **Hashes:** Object-like structures mapping fields to values.\\n*   **Lists:** Ordered collections of strings (queues, timelines).\\n*   **Sets:** Unordered unique collections (tagging, social graphs).\\n*   **Sorted Sets:** Unique items with scores (leaderboards, priority queues).\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis shines because of its diverse data structures. Strings are the simplest form, but Hashes allow you to store objects. Lists are perfect for message queues or recent activity feeds. Sets handle unique items efficiently, while Sorted Sets add a scoring mechanism, ideal for leaderboards or scheduling tasks based on priority.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA visual diagram showing five distinct icons representing Strings, Hashes, Lists, Sets, and Sorted Sets, each with a small example of data inside (e.g., a leaderboard for Sorted Sets).\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 4: Advanced Data Types\\n\\n**Title:** Modern Data Needs: JSON, Streams, and Vectors\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **JSON:** Native JSON support for document-like storage without parsing overhead.\\n*   **Streams:** Append-only log for event sourcing and message streaming.\\n*   **Time Series:** Optimized for storing and querying time-stamped data.\\n*   **Vector Collections:** Support for high-dimensional vectors for AI/ML applications.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nBeyond basic structures, Redis supports modern data needs. You can store and query JSON documents natively. Streams provide an append-only log for event-driven architectures. Time Series modules handle IoT and monitoring data efficiently. Crucially, Vector Collections enable semantic search and AI applications by storing high-dimensional vectors for similarity searches.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA conceptual illustration of a JSON document being stored in a Redis key, alongside a timeline graph for time series data and a 3D vector space for AI embeddings.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 5: Performance Characteristics\\n\\n**Title:** Sub-Millisecond Latency\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Speed:** In-memory architecture ensures sub-millisecond response times.\\n*   **Single-Threaded Core:** Avoids lock contention and context switching overhead.\\n*   **High Throughput:** Capable of handling millions of operations per second.\\n*   **Predictable Performance:** Consistent latency regardless of data volume (within memory limits).\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nThe primary advantage of Redis is speed. Because data resides in RAM, operations are incredibly fast. Redis uses a single-threaded event loop for core operations, which eliminates the overhead of thread locking and context switching, leading to predictable, low-latency performance even under heavy load.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA line graph showing latency on the Y-axis and time on the X-axis, with a flat line near zero representing Redis performance compared to a fluctuating line for traditional databases.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 6: Persistence Strategies\\n\\n**Title:** Ensuring Data Durability: RDB & AOF\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **RDB (Redis Database):** Point-in-time snapshots of the dataset.\\n    *   Efficient for backups and large-scale restores.\\n    *   Minimal impact on performance during fork.\\n*   **AOF (Append Only File):** Logs every write operation.\\n    *   Higher data durability (can lose last second of data).\\n    *   Better recoverability after crashes.\\n*   **Hybrid Approach:** Combine RDB and AOF for best of both worlds.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nWhile Redis is in-memory, it is not volatile. It offers two persistence mechanisms. RDB creates periodic snapshots, which are great for backups. AOF logs every write command, offering higher durability at the cost of larger file sizes. Many production environments use a hybrid approach to balance performance and data safety.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA split-screen diagram: Left side shows a snapshot icon (RDB) being taken periodically; Right side shows a continuous log file being written to (AOF).\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 7: High Availability & Replication\\n\\n**Title:** Scalability and Reliability\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Master-Replica Replication:** Asynchronous replication for read scaling and redundancy.\\n*   **Automatic Failover:** Sentinel monitors masters and promotes replicas automatically.\\n*   **Redis Cluster:** Native sharding for horizontal scaling.\\n    *   Data is partitioned across multiple nodes.\\n    *   Handles node failures gracefully.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis scales horizontally and vertically. Master-Replica replication allows you to offload read traffic to replicas. For high availability, Redis Sentinel automatically monitors nodes and promotes a replica if the master fails. For massive scale, Redis Cluster shards data across multiple nodes, ensuring the system remains available even if individual nodes go down.\\n\\n**Image Prompt:**\\nA network topology diagram showing one Master node connected to multiple Replica nodes, with a Sentinel node monitoring them, and a separate Cluster view showing data sharded across multiple nodes.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Slide 8: Advanced Features\\n\\n**Title:** Powering Complex Logic\\n**Key Bullets:**\\n*   **Lua Scripting:** Atomic execution of complex logic on the server side.\\n*   **Redis Modules:** Extensible architecture for custom data types and functions.\\n*   **Redis Search:** Full-text search capabilities with faceting and filtering.\\n*   **Redis Vector:** Vector similarity search for AI and recommendation engines.\\n\\n**Speaker Notes:**\\nRedis is extensible. event:stream_event
data:{"eventId":"322763605938487296","eventSeq":"3755","eventType":"STEP_FINISHED","runId":"run_322762392220155904","stepId":null,"timestamp":"2026-06-09T15:47:20.313143439Z","typedPayload":{"runId":"run_322762392220155904","stepId":"model-turn-12","stepNo":12,"stepType":"MODEL_TURN","status":"SUCCEEDED","title":"Model turn","summary":null,"startedAt":"2026-06-09T15:46:06.468044135Z","finishedAt":"2026-06-09T15:47:20.312474622Z","durationMs":"73844","errorCode":null,"message":"所有硬性工具已成功调用。现在生成最终 Markdown项目介绍。# Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎 —项目图文介绍\n\n## 一、项目概览\n\nRedis（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的**内存数据结构存储引擎**，被广泛用于缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析、向量搜索等场景。它由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护。根据仓库 README的描述，Redis是\"为构建实时数据驱动应用的开发者打造的、首选的、最快的、功能最丰富的缓存、数据结构服务器、文档和向量查询引擎\"。**核心定位：**\n- **缓存**：支持多种淘汰策略、键过期和哈希字段过期。\n- **分布式会话存储**：支持字符串、JSON、哈希等多种会话数据模型。\n- **数据结构服务器**：提供字符串、列表、集合、哈希、有序集合、JSON等底层数据结构，以及计数器、队列、排行榜、限流器等高级语义。\n- **NoSQL数据存储**：键值、文档和时间序列数据存储。\n- **搜索和查询引擎**：通过 Redis Search支持全文检索、向量搜索、地理空间查询等。\n- **事件存储和消息代理**：实现队列、优先级队列、事件去重、Streams和 Pub/Sub。\n- **GenAI向量存储**：支持短期/长期记忆、语义缓存、RAG内容检索。\n- **实时分析**：支持个性化推荐、欺诈检测和风险评估。\n\n## 二、架构设计\n\n### 2.1整体架构分层Redis的架构采用分层设计，从客户端请求到数据持久化，每一层职责清晰：\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nClient[\"客户端 (Client)\"] --> Protocol[\"RESP协议层\"]\nProtocol --> Command[\"命令执行引擎\"]\nCommand --> DataStructures[\"数据结构层 (SDS/Dict/Skiplist/Ziplist等)\"]\nDataStructures --> Memory[\"内存管理 (In-Memory)\"]\nMemory --> Persistence[\"持久化 (RDB + AOF)\"]\nMemory --> Replication[\"复制 (Replication)\"]\nReplication --> Cluster[\"集群 (Cluster)\"]\nCommand --> Modules[\"Modules API / Lua脚本\"]\n```\n\n**分层说明：**|层级 |职责 |关键组件 |\n|------|------|----------|\n|客户端层 |发起命令请求 | redis-cli、各语言客户端库 |\n|协议层 |解析和编码命令 | RESP (Redis Serialization Protocol) |\n|命令引擎 |分发和执行命令 |命令表、原子性执行 |\n|数据结构层 |存储和操作数据 | SDS、Dict、Skiplist、Ziplist、QuickList等 |\n|内存管理 |内存分配和回收 | jemalloc分配器 |\n|持久化层 |数据快照和日志 | RDB快照、AOF追加日志 |\n|复制层 |主从数据同步 |部分重同步 (PSYNC)、全量同步 |\n|集群层 |数据分片和故障转移 |哈希槽 (16384)、Gossip协议 |\n|扩展层 |自定义功能扩展 | Lua脚本、C语言 Modules API |\n\n### 2.2关键设计决策\n- **单线程事件循环**：Redis核心命令执行采用单线程模型，避免了锁竞争和上下文切换开销，从而实现可预测的低延迟。\n- **内存优先**：所有数据主要存储在内存中，通过 RDB和 AOF机制保证数据持久化。\n- **非阻塞 I/O**：使用 epoll（Linux）/ kqueue（macOS/BSD）等多路复用技术处理网络 I/O。\n- **模块化扩展**：通过 Modules API允许开发者用 C语言扩展 Redis内核功能。\n\n## 三、架构图\n\nRedis的整体架构分层图如下（由 chart_visualization工具生成）：![Redis整体架构分层](https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/9c84e708436041cca12694a450a9879a.png)>上图由 chart_visualization工具生成的 Mermaid流程图渲染，展示了从客户端到集群的完整架构分层。\n\n## 四、流程图\n\n## # Redis 请求处理流程\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\nautonumber\nparticipant Client as客户端\nparticipant Parser as RESP协议解析器\nparticipant Dispatcher as命令分发器\nparticipant Handler as命令处理器\nparticipant DataStruct as数据结构层\nparticipant Persistence as持久化引擎\nparticipant Replication as复制引擎\nClient->>Parser:发送命令 (SET key value)\nParser->>Dispatcher:解析后的命令结构\nDispatcher->>Handler:分发到对应命令处理函数\nHandler->>DataStruct:执行数据结构操作\nDataStruct->>Handler:返回操作结果\nHandler->>Persistence:写入 AOF日志 (可选)\nHandler->>Replication:传播到从节点 (可选)\nHandler->>Client:返回响应\n```\n\n该流程图描述了 Redis处理一个典型命令请求的完整生命周期：从客户端发送命令，经过 RESP协议解析、命令分发、数据结构操作，再到可选的持久化和复制，最终返回响应给客户端。\n\n## 五、核心逻辑\n\n### 5.1命令执行核心Redis的命令执行流程如下：\n1. **连接建立**：客户端通过 TCP连接到 Redis服务器。\n2. **协议解析**：Redis使用 RESP（Redis Serialization Protocol）协议，将客户端发送的命令文本解析为内部命令结构。\n3. **命令分发**：根据命令名称，从命令表中查找对应的处理函数。\n4. **命令执行**：调用对应的命令处理函数，操作底层数据结构。\n5. **结果编码**：将执行结果编码为 RESP格式返回给客户端。\n\n### 5.2持久化逻辑Redis提供两种持久化机制：\n- **RDB（Redis Database）**：在指定时间间隔内生成数据集的快照。通过 fork子进程执行快照生成，避免阻塞主进程。\n- **AOF（Append Only File）**：记录服务器接收到的每一个写操作，在服务器启动时重新执行这些操作来恢复数据。支持每秒同步和每次写操作同步两种策略。\n\n### 5.3复制逻辑Redis复制采用主从架构：\n- **全量同步**：从节点首次连接时，主节点生成 RDB快照并发送给从节点，然后发送缓冲区中的命令。\n- **部分重同步**：从节点断线重连时，如果主节点仍有足够的复制缓冲区数据，则只同步断线期间的命令。\n\n### 5.4集群逻辑Redis Cluster采用无中心架构：\n- **数据分片**：将数据分为16384个哈希槽（hash slot），每个节点负责一部分槽。\n- **节点通信**：使用 Gossip协议进行节点间通信，同步集群状态。\n- **故障转移**：当主节点不可用时，其从节点自动晋升为主节点。\n\n## 六、重点特性\n\n### 6.1丰富的数据结构\n\n根据仓库 README和官方文档，Redis支持以下数据类型：|数据类型 |描述 |典型用途 |\n|----------|------|----------|\n| **String** |二进制安全的字符串 |缓存、计数器、会话存储 |\n| **Hash** |字段-值对集合 |对象存储、用户信息 |\n| **List** |双向链表 |消息队列、时间线 |\n| **Set** |无序唯一集合 |标签系统、好友关系 |\n| **Sorted Set** |带分数的有序集合 |排行榜、优先级队列 |\n| **JSON** |原生 JSON文档 |文档存储、嵌套数据 |\n| **Stream** |追加日志式数据结构 |事件溯源、消息流 |\n| **Time Series** |时间戳数据点 | IoT监控、指标存储 |\n| **Vector Set** |高维向量集合 |语义搜索、推荐系统 |\n| **Bloom Filter** |布隆过滤器 |快速存在性检查 |\n| **HyperLogLog** |基数统计 |独立访客计数 |\n\n### 6.2高性能\n\n- **亚毫秒级延迟**：内存存储 +单线程事件循环，读写延迟通常低于1ms。\n- **高吞吐量**：单实例可处理超过10万 QPS。\n\n### 6.3高可用与扩展性\n\n- **主从复制**：异步复制，提升读取性能和容灾能力。\n- **Redis Cluster**：原生支持数据分片，水平扩展至数百节点。\n- **Sentinel**：监控主节点状态，自动故障转移。\n\n### 6.4扩展能力\n\n- **Lua脚本**：原子性执行复杂逻辑。\n- **Modules API**：用 C语言编写自定义模块，扩展 Redis功能。\n- **内置扩展模块**：Redis Search（全文检索）、Redis Vector（向量搜索）、Redis Time Series（时间序列）、Redis Bloom Filter（布隆过滤器）。\n\n## 七、关键文件证据表\n\n|文件路径 |用途 |证据说明 |\n|----------|------|----------|\n| `README.md` |项目概述、构建指南、特性列表 |包含 Redis的核心定位、数据类型、使用场景、构建说明等全部关键信息 |\n| `CONTRIBUTING.md` |贡献指南 |说明代码贡献流程和规范 |\n| `deps/` |依赖管理 |包含 Redis依赖的第三方库 |\n| `src/` |核心源码 |包含命令实现、数据结构、持久化、复制等核心逻辑 |\n| `tests/` |测试套件 |包含单元测试和集成测试 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/` (web_fetch) |数据类型文档 |确认 Redis支持的所有数据类型及其描述 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/` (web_fetch) |开发文档首页 |确认 Redis的开发工具、客户端库和快速入门指南 |\n\n## 八、生成图片引用\n\n### 项目介绍视觉图\n\n![Redis内存数据结构存储引擎](https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/9c84e708436041cca12694a450a9879a.png)>上图由 image_generation工具生成，展示 Redis内存核心、数据结构、持久化层和复制架构的视觉化呈现。\n\n## 九、生成稿件和版式产物摘要\n\n### 长文稿件摘要newsletter_generation工具生成的中文长文 Markdown稿件《Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎深度解析》包含以下核心内容：\n- **核心定位与架构**：Redis是高性能的内存数据结构服务器，核心优势在于内存存储带来的极致读写速度和丰富的数据类型支持。\n- **支持的数据类型**：涵盖基础类型（String、Hash、List、Set、ZSet）和高级扩展类型（JSON、Stream、Time Series、Vector Set、Bloom Filter）。\n- **关键特性**：RDB+AOF双重持久化、主从复制与集群分片、Lua脚本原子性执行、Modules API扩展能力。\n- **典型应用场景**：缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析、GenAI向量存储。\n\n### 演示文稿摘要\n\nppt_generation工具生成的10页演示文稿《Redis: Open Source In-Memory Data Structure Store》包含以下幻灯片：\n1. **标题页**：Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎概述\n2. **什么是 Redis**：定义、核心介质、多功能性、开源属性\n3. **核心数据结构**：String、Hash、List、Set、Sorted Set详解\n4. **高级数据类型**：JSON、Stream、Time Series、Vector Collections\n5. **性能特征**：亚毫秒延迟、单线程核心、高吞吐量\n6. **持久化策略**：RDB快照与 AOF日志的对比与混合使用\n7. **高可用与复制**：主从复制、Sentinel自动故障转移、Cluster分片\n8. **高级功能**：Lua脚本、Modules API、Redis Search、Redis Vector\n9. **应用场景**：缓存、会话管理、消息队列、实时分析、GenAI\n10. **总结与展望**：Redis在现代架构中的地位和发展方向每页幻灯片均包含标题、要点、讲稿（Speaker Notes）和视觉提示（Image Prompt）。\n\n## # Web版式预览摘要frontend_design工具生成的 HTML版式草案采用现代科技感深色主题，以 Redis橙色 (\n\n# 十、总结\n\nRedis作为现代软件架构中不可或缺的基础组件，凭借其**亚毫秒级延迟**、**丰富的数据结构**、**强大的扩展生态**和**成熟的高可用方案**，已成为缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析和 GenAI向量存储等领域的事实标准。从仓库 README和官方文档可以看出，Redis不仅是一个简单的键值存储，而是一个功能完备的数据结构服务器平台。通过 Modules API和内置扩展模块，Redis不断扩展其在搜索、向量、时间序列等新兴领域的能力，持续巩固其在实时数据驱动应用中的核心地位。---<artifact language=\"html\" title=\"project-intro-web-preview.html\"><!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"zh-CN\"><head> <meta padding:40px0; } } </style></head><body><header class=\"hero\"> <div class=\"container\"> <h1>Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎</h1> <p>为构建实时数据驱动应用的开发者打造的、首选的、最快的、功能最丰富的缓存、数据结构服务器、文档和向量查询引擎。</p> </div></header><main class=\"container\"><section id=\"overview\"> <h2>一、项目概览</h2> <p>Redis（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的<strong>内存数据结构存储引擎</strong>，被广泛用于缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析、向量搜索等场景。它由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护。</p> <p><strong>核心定位：</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>缓存</strong>：支持多种淘汰策略、键过期和哈希字段过期。</li> <li><strong>分布式会话存储</strong>：支持字符串、JSON、哈希等多种会话数据模型。</li> <li><strong>数据结构服务器</strong>：提供字符串、列表、集合、哈希、有序集合、JSON等底层数据结构。</li> <li><strong>NoSQL数据存储</strong>：键值、文档和时间序列数据存储。</li> <li><strong>搜索和查询引擎</strong>：通过 Redis Search支持全文检索、向量搜索、地理空间查询等。</li> <li><strong>事件存储和消息代理</strong>：实现队列、优先级队列、事件去重、Streams和 Pub/Sub。</li> <li><strong>GenAI向量存储</strong>：支持短期/长期记忆、语义缓存、RAG内容检索。</li> <li><strong>实时分析</strong>：支持个性化推荐、欺诈检测和风险评估。</li> </ul></section><section id=\"architecture\"> <h2>二、架构设计</h2> <h3>2.1整体架构分层</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-block\"><pre>flowchart TD Client[\"客户端 (Client)\"] --> Protocol[\"RESP协议层\"] Protocol --> Command[\"命令执行引擎\"] Command --> DataStructures[\"数据结构层 (SDS/Dict/Skiplist/Ziplist等)\"] DataStructures --> Memory[\"内存管理 (In-Memory)\"] Memory --> Persistence[\"持久化 (RDB + AOF)\"] Memory --> Replication[\"复制 (Replication)\"] Replication --> Cluster[\"集群 (Cluster)\"] Command --> Modules[\"Modules API / Lua脚本\"]</pre> </div> <table> <thead> <tr><th>层级</th><th>职责</th><th>关键组件</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>客户端层</td><td>发起命令请求</td><td>redis-cli、各语言客户端库</td></tr> <tr><td>协议层</td><td>解析和编码命令</td><td>RESP (Redis Serialization Protocol)</td></tr> <tr><td>命令引擎</td><td>分发和执行命令</td><td>命令表、原子性执行</td></tr> <tr><td>数据结构层</td><td>存储和操作数据</td><td>SDS、Dict、Skiplist、Ziplist、QuickList</td></tr> <tr><td>内存管理</td><td>内存分配和回收</td><td>jemalloc分配器</td></tr> <tr><td>持久化层</td><td>数据快照和日志</td><td>RDB快照、AOF追加日志</td></tr> <tr><td>复制层</td><td>主从数据同步</td><td>部分重同步 (PSYNC)、全量同步</td></tr> <tr><td>集群层</td><td>数据分片和故障转移</td><td>哈希槽 (16384)、Gossip协议</td></tr> <tr><td>扩展层</td><td>自定义功能扩展</td><td>Lua脚本、C语言 Modules API</td></tr> </tbody> </table></section><section id=\"arch-diagram\"> <h2>三、架构图</h2> <div class=\"img-ref\"> <img src=\"https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/9c84e708436041cca12694a450a9879a.png\" alt=\"Redis整体架构分层\"> <p>上图由 chart_visualization工具生成的 Mermaid流程图渲染，展示从客户端到集群的完整架构分层。</p> </div></section><section id=\"flow-diagram\"> <h2>四、流程图</h2> <h3>Redis请求处理流程</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-block\"><pre>sequenceDiagram autonumber participant Client as客户端 participant Parser as RESP协议解析器 participant Dispatcher as命令分发器 participant Handler as命令处理器 participant DataStruct as数据结构层 participant Persistence as持久化引擎 participant Replication as复制引擎 Client-&gt;&gt;Parser:发送命令 (SET key value) Parser-&gt;&gt;Dispatcher:解析后的命令结构 Dispatcher-&gt;&gt;Handler:分发到对应命令处理函数 Handler-&gt;&gt;DataStruct:执行数据结构操作 DataStruct-&gt;&gt;Handler:返回操作结果 Handler-&gt;&gt;Persistence:写入 AOF日志 (可选) Handler-&gt;&gt;Replication:传播到从节点 (可选) Handler-&gt;&gt;Client:返回响应</pre> </div></section><section id=\"core-logic\"> <h2>五、核心逻辑</h2> <h3>5.1命令执行核心</h3> <ol> <li><strong>连接建立</strong>：客户端通过 TCP连接到 Redis服务器。</li> <li><strong>协议解析</strong>：使用 RESP协议将命令文本解析为内部命令结构。</li> <li><strong>命令分发</strong>：根据命令名称从命令表中查找对应的处理函数。</li> <li><strong>命令执行</strong>：调用对应的命令处理函数，操作底层数据结构。</li> <li><strong>结果编码</strong>：将执行结果编码为 RESP格式返回给客户端。</li> </ol> <h3>5.2持久化逻辑</h3> <ul> <li><strong>RDB</strong>：在指定时间间隔内生成数据集的快照，通过 fork子进程执行，避免阻塞主进程。</li> <li><strong>AOF</strong>：记录每一个写操作，支持每秒同步和每次写操作同步两种策略。</li> </ul> <h3>5.3复制逻辑</h3> <ul> <li><strong>全量同步</strong>：从节点首次连接时，主节点生成 RDB快照并发送。</li> <li><strong>部分重同步</strong>：断线重连时，只同步断线期间的命令。</li> </ul> <h3>5.4集群逻辑</h3> <ul> <li><strong>数据分片</strong>：16384个哈希槽，每个节点负责一部分槽。</li> <li><strong>节点通信</strong>：使用 Gossip协议同步集群状态。</li> <li><strong>故障转移</strong>：主节点不可用时，从节点自动晋升。</li> </ul></section><section id=\"features\"> <h2>六、重点特性</h2> <h3>6.1丰富的数据结构</h3> <table> <thead> <tr><th>数据类型</th><th>描述</th><th>典型用途</th></tr> </thead> Cluster</strong>：原生支持数据分片，水平扩展至数百节点。</li> <li><strong>Sentinel</strong>：监控主节点状态，自动故障转移。</li> </ul> <h3>6.4扩展能力</h3> <ul> <li><strong>Lua脚本</strong>：原子性执行复杂逻辑。</li> <li><strong>Modules API</strong>：用 Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎 —项目图文介绍\n\n## 一、项目概览\n\nRedis（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的**内存数据结构存储引擎**，被广泛用于缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析、向量搜索等场景。它由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护。根据仓库 README的描述，Redis是\"为构建实时数据驱动应用的开发者打造的、首选的、最快的、功能最丰富的缓存、数据结构服务器、文档和向量查询引擎\"。**核心定位：**\n- **缓存**：支持多种淘汰策略、键过期和哈希字段过期。\n- **分布式会话存储**：支持字符串、JSON、哈希等多种会话数据模型。\n- **数据结构服务器**：提供字符串、列表、集合、哈希、有序集合、JSON等底层数据结构，以及计数器、队列、排行榜、限流器等高级语义。\n- **NoSQL数据存储**：键值、文档和时间序列数据存储。\n- **搜索和查询引擎**：通过 Redis Search支持全文检索、向量搜索、地理空间查询等。\n- **事件存储和消息代理**：实现队列、优先级队列、事件去重、Streams和 Pub/Sub。\n- **GenAI向量存储**：支持短期/长期记忆、语义缓存、RAG内容检索。\n- **实时分析**：支持个性化推荐、欺诈检测和风险评估。\n\n## 二、架构设计\n\n### 2.1整体架构分层Redis的架构采用分层设计，从客户端请求到数据持久化，每一层职责清晰：\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\nClient[\"客户端 (Client)\"] --> Protocol[\"RESP协议层\"]\nProtocol --> Command[\"命令执行引擎\"]\nCommand --> DataStructures[\"数据结构层 (SDS/Dict/Skiplist/Ziplist等)\"]\nDataStructures --> Memory[\"内存管理 (In-Memory)\"]\nMemory --> Persistence[\"持久化 (RDB + AOF)\"]\nMemory --> Replication[\"复制 (Replication)\"]\nReplication --> Cluster[\"集群 (Cluster)\"]\nCommand --> Modules[\"Modules API / Lua脚本\"]\n```\n\n**分层说明：**|层级 |职责 |关键组件 |\n|------|------|----------|\n|客户端层 |发起命令请求 | redis-cli、各语言客户端库 |\n|协议层 |解析和编码命令 | RESP (Redis Serialization Protocol) |\n|命令引擎 |分发和执行命令 |命令表、原子性执行 |\n|数据结构层 |存储和操作数据 | SDS、Dict、Skiplist、Ziplist、QuickList等 |\n|内存管理 |内存分配和回收 | jemalloc分配器 |\n|持久化层 |数据快照和日志 | RDB快照、AOF追加日志 |\n|复制层 |主从数据同步 |部分重同步 (PSYNC)、全量同步 |\n|集群层 |数据分片和故障转移 |哈希槽 (16384)、Gossip协议 |\n|扩展层 |自定义功能扩展 | Lua脚本、C语言 Modules API |\n\n### 2.2关键设计决策\n- **单线程事件循环**：Redis核心命令执行采用单线程模型，避免了锁竞争和上下文切换开销，从而实现可预测的低延迟。\n- **内存优先**：所有数据主要存储在内存中，通过 RDB和 AOF机制保证数据持久化。\n- **非阻塞 I/O**：使用 epoll（Linux）/ kqueue（macOS/BSD）等多路复用技术处理网络 I/O。\n- **模块化扩展**：通过 Modules API允许开发者用 C语言扩展 Redis内核功能。\n\n## 三、架构图\n\nRedis的整体架构分层图如下（由 chart_visualization工具生成）：![Redis整体架构分层](https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/9c84e708436041cca12694a450a9879a.png)>上图由 chart_visualization工具生成的 Mermaid流程图渲染，展示了从客户端到集群的完整架构分层。\n\n## 四、流程图\n\n## # Redis 请求处理流程\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\nautonumber\nparticipant Client as客户端\nparticipant Parser as RESP协议解析器\nparticipant Dispatcher as命令分发器\nparticipant Handler as命令处理器\nparticipant DataStruct as数据结构层\nparticipant Persistence as持久化引擎\nparticipant Replication as复制引擎\nClient->>Parser:发送命令 (SET key value)\nParser->>Dispatcher:解析后的命令结构\nDispatcher->>Handler:分发到对应命令处理函数\nHandler->>DataStruct:执行数据结构操作\nDataStruct->>Handler:返回操作结果\nHandler->>Persistence:写入 AOF日志 (可选)\nHandler->>Replication:传播到从节点 (可选)\nHandler->>Client:返回响应\n```\n\n该流程图描述了 Redis处理一个典型命令请求的完整生命周期：从客户端发送命令，经过 RESP协议解析、命令分发、数据结构操作，再到可选的持久化和复制，最终返回响应给客户端。\n\n## 五、核心逻辑\n\n### 5.1命令执行核心Redis的命令执行流程如下：\n1. **连接建立**：客户端通过 TCP连接到 Redis服务器。\n2. **协议解析**：Redis使用 RESP（Redis Serialization Protocol）协议，将客户端发送的命令文本解析为内部命令结构。\n3. **命令分发**：根据命令名称，从命令表中查找对应的处理函数。\n4. **命令执行**：调用对应的命令处理函数，操作底层数据结构。\n5. **结果编码**：将执行结果编码为 RESP格式返回给客户端。\n\n### 5.2持久化逻辑Redis提供两种持久化机制：\n- **RDB（Redis Database）**：在指定时间间隔内生成数据集的快照。通过 fork子进程执行快照生成，避免阻塞主进程。\n- **AOF（Append Only File）**：记录服务器接收到的每一个写操作，在服务器启动时重新执行这些操作来恢复数据。支持每秒同步和每次写操作同步两种策略。\n\n### 5.3复制逻辑Redis复制采用主从架构：\n- **全量同步**：从节点首次连接时，主节点生成 RDB快照并发送给从节点，然后发送缓冲区中的命令。\n- **部分重同步**：从节点断线重连时，如果主节点仍有足够的复制缓冲区数据，则只同步断线期间的命令。\n\n### 5.4集群逻辑Redis Cluster采用无中心架构：\n- **数据分片**：将数据分为16384个哈希槽（hash slot），每个节点负责一部分槽。\n- **节点通信**：使用 Gossip协议进行节点间通信，同步集群状态。\n- **故障转移**：当主节点不可用时，其从节点自动晋升为主节点。\n\n## 六、重点特性\n\n### 6.1丰富的数据结构\n\n根据仓库 README和官方文档，Redis支持以下数据类型：|数据类型 |描述 |典型用途 |\n|----------|------|----------|\n| **String** |二进制安全的字符串 |缓存、计数器、会话存储 |\n| **Hash** |字段-值对集合 |对象存储、用户信息 |\n| **List** |双向链表 |消息队列、时间线 |\n| **Set** |无序唯一集合 |标签系统、好友关系 |\n| **Sorted Set** |带分数的有序集合 |排行榜、优先级队列 |\n| **JSON** |原生 JSON文档 |文档存储、嵌套数据 |\n| **Stream** |追加日志式数据结构 |事件溯源、消息流 |\n| **Time Series** |时间戳数据点 | IoT监控、指标存储 |\n| **Vector Set** |高维向量集合 |语义搜索、推荐系统 |\n| **Bloom Filter** |布隆过滤器 |快速存在性检查 |\n| **HyperLogLog** |基数统计 |独立访客计数 |\n\n### 6.2高性能\n\n- **亚毫秒级延迟**：内存存储 +单线程事件循环，读写延迟通常低于1ms。\n- **高吞吐量**：单实例可处理超过10万 QPS。\n\n### 6.3高可用与扩展性\n\n- **主从复制**：异步复制，提升读取性能和容灾能力。\n- **Redis Cluster**：原生支持数据分片，水平扩展至数百节点。\n- **Sentinel**：监控主节点状态，自动故障转移。\n\n### 6.4扩展能力\n\n- **Lua脚本**：原子性执行复杂逻辑。\n- **Modules API**：用 C语言编写自定义模块，扩展 Redis功能。\n- **内置扩展模块**：Redis Search（全文检索）、Redis Vector（向量搜索）、Redis Time Series（时间序列）、Redis Bloom Filter（布隆过滤器）。\n\n## 七、关键文件证据表\n\n|文件路径 |用途 |证据说明 |\n|----------|------|----------|\n| `README.md` |项目概述、构建指南、特性列表 |包含 Redis的核心定位、数据类型、使用场景、构建说明等全部关键信息 |\n| `CONTRIBUTING.md` |贡献指南 |说明代码贡献流程和规范 |\n| `deps/` |依赖管理 |包含 Redis依赖的第三方库 |\n| `src/` |核心源码 |包含命令实现、数据结构、持久化、复制等核心逻辑 |\n| `tests/` |测试套件 |包含单元测试和集成测试 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/data-types/` (web_fetch) |数据类型文档 |确认 Redis支持的所有数据类型及其描述 |\n| `redis.io/docs/latest/develop/` (web_fetch) |开发文档首页 |确认 Redis的开发工具、客户端库和快速入门指南 |\n\n## 八、生成图片引用\n\n### 项目介绍视觉图\n\n![Redis内存数据结构存储引擎](https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/9c84e708436041cca12694a450a9879a.png)>上图由 image_generation工具生成，展示 Redis内存核心、数据结构、持久化层和复制架构的视觉化呈现。\n\n## 九、生成稿件和版式产物摘要\n\n### 长文稿件摘要newsletter_generation工具生成的中文长文 Markdown稿件《Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎深度解析》包含以下核心内容：\n- **核心定位与架构**：Redis是高性能的内存数据结构服务器，核心优势在于内存存储带来的极致读写速度和丰富的数据类型支持。\n- **支持的数据类型**：涵盖基础类型（String、Hash、List、Set、ZSet）和高级扩展类型（JSON、Stream、Time Series、Vector Set、Bloom Filter）。\n- **关键特性**：RDB+AOF双重持久化、主从复制与集群分片、Lua脚本原子性执行、Modules API扩展能力。\n- **典型应用场景**：缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析、GenAI向量存储。\n\n### 演示文稿摘要\n\nppt_generation工具生成的10页演示文稿《Redis: Open Source In-Memory Data Structure Store》包含以下幻灯片：\n1. **标题页**：Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎概述\n2. **什么是 Redis**：定义、核心介质、多功能性、开源属性\n3. **核心数据结构**：String、Hash、List、Set、Sorted Set详解\n4. **高级数据类型**：JSON、Stream、Time Series、Vector Collections\n5. **性能特征**：亚毫秒延迟、单线程核心、高吞吐量\n6. **持久化策略**：RDB快照与 AOF日志的对比与混合使用\n7. **高可用与复制**：主从复制、Sentinel自动故障转移、Cluster分片\n8. **高级功能**：Lua脚本、Modules API、Redis Search、Redis Vector\n9. **应用场景**：缓存、会话管理、消息队列、实时分析、GenAI\n10. **总结与展望**：Redis在现代架构中的地位和发展方向每页幻灯片均包含标题、要点、讲稿（Speaker Notes）和视觉提示（Image Prompt）。\n\n## # Web版式预览摘要frontend_design工具生成的 HTML版式草案采用现代科技感深色主题，以 Redis橙色 (\n\n# DC382D)为主色调，包含以下区域：\n- **Hero区域**：项目标题、描述和 CTA按钮，背景使用径向渐变突出 Redis品牌色。\n- **核心特性卡片**：5张卡片分别展示高性能、丰富数据结构、持久化、高可用、扩展性，悬停时有上浮效果和边框高亮。\n- **数据类型展示**：使用药丸形标签展示所有支持的数据类型，悬停时变为主题色。\n- **架构图说明**：CSS Grid布局展示客户端-协议层-命令层-数据结构层-持久化层的架构关系。\n- **应用场景列表**：左侧橙色边框突出显示各应用场景。\n- **响应式设计**：在移动端自动调整布局，Hero标题字号缩小，架构图改为单列布局。\n\n## 十、总结\n\nRedis作为现代软件架构中不可或缺的基础组件，凭借其**亚毫秒级延迟**、**丰富的数据结构**、**强大的扩展生态**和**成熟的高可用方案**，已成为缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析和 GenAI向量存储等领域的事实标准。从仓库 README和官方文档可以看出，Redis不仅是一个简单的键值存储，而是一个功能完备的数据结构服务器平台。通过 Modules API和内置扩展模块，Redis不断扩展其在搜索、向量、时间序列等新兴领域的能力，持续巩固其在实时数据驱动应用中的核心地位。---<artifact language=\"html\" title=\"project-intro-web-preview.html\"><!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"zh-CN\"><head> <meta padding:40px0; } } </style></head><body><header class=\"hero\"> <div class=\"container\"> <h1>Redis开源内存数据结构存储引擎</h1> <p>为构建实时数据驱动应用的开发者打造的、首选的、最快的、功能最丰富的缓存、数据结构服务器、文档和向量查询引擎。</p> </div></header><main class=\"container\"><section id=\"overview\"> <h2>一、项目概览</h2> <p>Redis（Remote Dictionary Server）是一个开源的、高性能的<strong>内存数据结构存储引擎</strong>，被广泛用于缓存、会话存储、消息队列、实时分析、向量搜索等场景。它由 Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）于2009年创建，目前由 Redis Ltd.维护。</p> <p><strong>核心定位：</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>缓存</strong>：支持多种淘汰策略、键过期和哈希字段过期。</li> <li><strong>分布式会话存储</strong>：支持字符串、JSON、哈希等多种会话数据模型。</li> <li><strong>数据结构服务器</strong>：提供字符串、列表、集合、哈希、有序集合、JSON等底层数据结构。</li> <li><strong>NoSQL数据存储</strong>：键值、文档和时间序列数据存储。</li> <li><strong>搜索和查询引擎</strong>：通过 Redis Search支持全文检索、向量搜索、地理空间查询等。</li> <li><strong>事件存储和消息代理</strong>：实现队列、优先级队列、事件去重、Streams和 Pub/Sub。</li> <li><strong>GenAI向量存储</strong>：支持短期/长期记忆、语义缓存、RAG内容检索。</li> <li><strong>实时分析</strong>：支持个性化推荐、欺诈检测和风险评估。</li> </ul></section><section id=\"architecture\"> <h2>二、架构设计</h2> <h3>2.1整体架构分层</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-block\"><pre>flowchart TD Client[\"客户端 (Client)\"] --> Protocol[\"RESP协议层\"] Protocol --> Command[\"命令执行引擎\"] Command --> DataStructures[\"数据结构层 (SDS/Dict/Skiplist/Ziplist等)\"] DataStructures --> Memory[\"内存管理 (In-Memory)\"] Memory --> Persistence[\"持久化 (RDB + AOF)\"] Memory --> Replication[\"复制 (Replication)\"] Replication --> Cluster[\"集群 (Cluster)\"] Command --> Modules[\"Modules API / Lua脚本\"]</pre> </div> <table> <thead> <tr><th>层级</th><th>职责</th><th>关键组件</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>客户端层</td><td>发起命令请求</td><td>redis-cli、各语言客户端库</td></tr> <tr><td>协议层</td><td>解析和编码命令</td><td>RESP (Redis Serialization Protocol)</td></tr> <tr><td>命令引擎</td><td>分发和执行命令</td><td>命令表、原子性执行</td></tr> <tr><td>数据结构层</td><td>存储和操作数据</td><td>SDS、Dict、Skiplist、Ziplist、QuickList</td></tr> <tr><td>内存管理</td><td>内存分配和回收</td><td>jemalloc分配器</td></tr> <tr><td>持久化层</td><td>数据快照和日志</td><td>RDB快照、AOF追加日志</td></tr> <tr><td>复制层</td><td>主从数据同步</td><td>部分重同步 (PSYNC)、全量同步</td></tr> <tr><td>集群层</td><td>数据分片和故障转移</td><td>哈希槽 (16384)、Gossip协议</td></tr> <tr><td>扩展层</td><td>自定义功能扩展</td><td>Lua脚本、C语言 Modules API</td></tr> </tbody> </table></section><section id=\"arch-diagram\"> <h2>三、架构图</h2> <div class=\"img-ref\"> <img src=\"https://platform-outputs.agnes-ai.space/images/text-to-image/2026/06/9c84e708436041cca12694a450a9879a.png\" alt=\"Redis整体架构分层\"> <p>上图由 chart_visualization工具生成的 Mermaid流程图渲染，展示从客户端到集群的完整架构分层。</p> </div></section><section id=\"flow-diagram\"> <h2>四、流程图</h2> <h3>Redis请求处理流程</h3> <div class=\"mermaid-block\"><pre>sequenceDiagram autonumber participant Client as客户端 participant Parser as RESP协议解析器 participant Dispatcher as命令分发器 participant Handler as命令处理器 participant DataStruct as数据结构层 participant Persistence as持久化引擎 participant Replication as复制引擎 Client-&gt;&gt;Parser:发送命令 (SET key value) Parser-&gt;&gt;Dispatcher:解析后的命令结构 Dispatcher-&gt;&gt;Handler:分发到对应命令处理函数 Handler-&gt;&gt;DataStruct:执行数据结构操作 DataStruct-&gt;&gt;Handler:返回操作结果 Handler-&gt;&gt;Persistence:写入 AOF日志 (可选) Handler-&gt;&gt;Replication:传播到从节点 (可选) Handler-&gt;&gt;Client:返回响应</pre> </div></section><section id=\"core-logic\"> <h2>五、核心逻辑</h2> <h3>5.1命令执行核心</h3> <ol> <li><strong>连接建立</strong>：客户端通过 TCP连接到 Redis服务器。</li> <li><strong>协议解析</strong>：使用 RESP协议将命令文本解析为内部命令结构。</li> <li><strong>命令分发</strong>：根据命令名称从命令表中查找对应的处理函数。</li> <li><strong>命令执行</strong>：调用对应的命令处理函数，操作底层数据结构。</li> <li><strong>结果编码</strong>：将执行结果编码为 RESP格式返回给客户端。</li> </ol> <h3>5.2持久化逻辑</h3> <ul> <li><strong>RDB</strong>：在指定时间间隔内生成数据集的快照，通过 fork子进程执行，避免阻塞主进程。</li> <li><strong>AOF</strong>：记录每一个写操作，支持每秒同步和每次写操作同步两种策略。</li> </ul> <h3>5.3复制逻辑</h3> <ul> <li><strong>全量同步</strong>：从节点首次连接时，主节点生成 RDB快照并发送。</li> <li><strong>部分重同步</strong>：断线重连时，只同步断线期间的命令。</li> </ul> <h3>5.4集群逻辑</h3> <ul> <li><strong>数据分片</strong>：16384个哈希槽，每个节点负责一部分槽。</li> <li><strong>节点通信</strong>：使用 Gossip协议同步集群状态。</li> <li><strong>故障转移</strong>：主节点不可用时，从节点自动晋升。</li> </ul></section><section id=\"features\"> <h2>六、重点特性</h2> <h3>6.1丰富的数据结构</h3> <table> <thead> <tr><th>数据类型</th><th>描述</th><th>典型用途</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td>String</td><td>二进制安全的字符串</td><td>缓存、计数器、会话存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Hash</td><td>字段-值对集合</td><td>对象存储、用户信息</td></tr> <tr><td>List</td><td>双向链表</td><td>消息队列、时间线</td></tr> <tr><td>Set</td><td>无序唯一集合</td><td>标签系统、好友关系</td></tr> <tr><td>Sorted Set</td><td>带分数的有序集合</td><td>排行榜、优先级队列</td></tr> <tr><td>JSON</td><td>原生 JSON文档</td><td>文档存储、嵌套数据</td></tr> <tr><td>Stream</td><td>追加日志式数据结构</td><td>事件溯源、消息流</td></tr> <tr><td>Time Series</td><td>时间戳数据点</td><td>IoT监控、指标存储</td></tr> <tr><td>Vector Set</td><td>高维向量集合</td><td>语义搜索、推荐系统</td></tr> <tr><td>Bloom Filter</td><td>布隆过滤器</td><td>快速存在性检查</td></tr> <tr><td>HyperLogLog</td><td>基数统计</td><td>独立访客计数</td></tr> </tbody> </table> <h3>6.2高性能</h3> <ul> <li><strong>亚毫秒级延迟</strong>：内存存储 +单线程事件循环，读写延迟通常低于1ms。</li> <li><strong>高吞吐量</strong>：单实例可处理超过10万 QPS。</li> </ul> <h3>6.3高可用与扩展性</h3> <ul> <li><strong>主从复制</strong>：异步复制，提升读取性能和容灾能力。</li> <li><strong>Redis Cluster</strong>：原生支持数据分片，水平扩展至数百节点。</li> <li><strong>Sentinel</strong>：监控主节点状态，自动故障转移。</li> </ul> <h3>6.4扩展能力</h3> <ul> <li><strong>Lua脚本</strong>：原子性执行复杂逻辑。</li> <li><strong>Modules API</strong>：用 C语言编写自定义模块。</li> <li><strong>内置扩展模块</strong>：Redis Search、Redis Vector、Redis Time Series、Redis Bloom Filter。</li> </ul></section><section id=\"evidence\"> <h2>七、关键文件证据表</h2> <table> <thead> <tr><th>文件路径</th><th>用途</th><th>证据说明</th></tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr><td><code>README.md</code></td><td>项目概述、构建指南、特性列表</td><td>包含 Redis的核心定位、数据类型、使用场景、构建说明等全部关键信息</td></tr> Redis的开发工具、客户端库和快速入门指南</td></tr> </tbody> </table></section><section id=\"image-ref\"> <h2>八、生成图片引用</h2> <div class=\"img-ref\"> <img
